title: Sundial diagnostic envelope
description: v0.1 of the `sundial check --json` output envelope. The envelope IS the product surface; its shape is treated as API. Versioned independently of the tool. Spec of record: vault talk/diagnostic-envelope.md.

sundial.description: Envelope schema version. Independent of the tool version.

at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the run was produced. Excluded from determinism guarantees.

mode.description: text = ordinary TSX codebase (pillar 1). graph = graph-canonical project (pillar 3); enables `target` addressing.

diagnostics.description: Deterministically ordered: by span.file, then span.range[0], then code.

bySource.description: Diagnostic count keyed by source (e.g. { ts: 3, sundial: 1 }).

code.description: Stable code. TS diagnostics pass through with their native code (e.g. TS2322). Sundial rules are SND-namespaced, zero-padded (SND0001). Append-only, never reused, meaning frozen.

source.description: ts = passed through from the TypeScript toolchain. sundial = a framework-level SND rule.

severity.description: Mirrors the TS language service's three tiers.

message.description: Human-readable prose. Advisory only: nothing agent-relevant lives only here; the authoritative form is `facts`.

target.description: Semantic address into the graph. Absent in text mode. Both span.contentHash and target.nodeHash provide staleness detection.

facts.description: Structured expected/observed data (e.g. { declared: [], observed: ["net"] }). Authoritative machine surface.

docs.description: Stable rule URI, resolved by the skills doc (e.g. sundial://rules/SND0001).

file.description: Repo-relative POSIX path. On graph-canonical projects this addresses the projection.

range.description: [start, end] UTF-16 character offsets into the file, half-open.

contentHash.description: Hash of the addressed file's content at run time. Staleness guard: a later reader can detect the file changed since this diagnostic was produced.

repair.description: A checker-suggested patch. Repairs share the pillar-3 patch vocabulary. In text mode a repair lowers to `edits` (textEdit form); the semantic `op` is reserved for when the patch API lands.

id.description: Stable-within-run identifier for the repair (e.g. the TS fixName or an SND repair id).

safety.description: safe = mechanical, meaning-preserving. review = plausibly changes behavior; a human/agent should confirm. unsafe = known to change behavior.

op.description: Reserved: the semantic patch operation, once the pillar-3 patch vocabulary exists. Absent in text mode v0.1.

edits.description: Lowered text-edit form. Applied together as one atomic repair.

range.description: [start, end] UTF-16 character offsets, half-open. An insertion has start == end.
